title: peucedanum verticillare
intro: Peucedanum verticillare, also known as fern-leaved or fern-leafed carrot, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Eastern Asia and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Peucedanum verticillare include its ability to reduce fever, relieve congestion, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
